paragraph: The single-family market in Teaneck is competitive but not frenzied. With 113 sales over seven months at a median of $660,000, you're looking at steady volume and a sale-to-list ratio right at 1.00, meaning homes are selling for asking price on average. About 55 percent of single-family homes sold above list, and the median time on market sits at 26 days. Priced homes are moving, but sellers aren't dictating terms. By contrast, the condo and townhouse category is smaller with only 16 sales at a median of $533,000, though these units are moving faster at a 20-day median and 69 percent selling above asking. Co-ops sit at the entry point with a median of $195,000, but volume is sparse at 6 sales over the period. These units spend an average of 44.5 days on market and sell slightly below asking, suggesting buyers have leverage in this tier. If you're comparing tiers, the deciding factor isn't market heat. It's whether you want the space and grounds of a single-family, the low-maintenance lifestyle of a condo, or the lower price point of a co-op.
